mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects rising aggregate labor income that supports consumption and corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 50 percent of observations during periods of rising PAYEMS over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5.0 percent represents a restrictive monetary stance that raises borrowing costs and compresses equity valuations through a higher discount rate, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50 percent of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0 percent, or Rank IC is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of elevated consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) and a high federal funds rate signals an aggressively restrictive monetary policy that overtightens financial conditions and squeezes corporate margins, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50 percent of observations when both CPIAUCSL is above its 12-month moving average and FEDFUNDS is above 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: During a late-cycle expansion, a rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) of more than 0.3 percentage points within three months signals deteriorating labor demand that precedes earnings downgrades and recession risk, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 60 percent of triggered instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points within three months during late-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) below 1,400 thousand units indicate a contraction in residential construction and downstream durable goods demand that reduces aggregate output expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50 percent of observations when HOUST is below 1,400, or the signal Rank IC is above zero over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) grow faster than industrial production (INDPRO), the gap signals demand is being satisfied through inventory drawdowns and imports rather than domestic output, squeezing margins and portending a production catch-down that drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 55 percent of observations when RSAFS month-over-month growth exceeds INDPRO month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in industrial production (INDPRO) of more than 1.0 index point within two months signals rising capacity utilization and manufacturing pricing power that boosts earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO increases > 1.0 index point in 2 months
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are negative in more than 50 percent of triggered instances, or the walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) to a month-over-month change below 0.2 percent reduces policy uncertainty and supports equity multiple expansion by lowering the expected path of discount rates, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 50 percent of triggered instances when CPIAUCSL month-over-month change is below 0.2 percent.
